items.question: In which year did the World Heritage framework add a category for cultural landscapes?
items.explanation: The UNESCO convention timeline notes cultural landscapes were added in 1992.

items.question: Properties considered for the World Heritage List must demonstrate what kind of value?
items.explanation: UNESCO’s World Heritage Convention overview centers on outstanding universal value for listed properties.

items.question: Sustainable Development Goal target 11.4 calls for strengthened efforts to protect and safeguard what?
items.correctAnswer: the world’s cultural and natural heritage
items.explanation: UN SDG 11 targets list 11.4 on protecting cultural and natural heritage.

items.question: The Budapest Declaration on World Heritage, expanding strategic objectives, was adopted during which anniversary of the Convention?
items.explanation: UNESCO’s convention timeline links the Budapest Declaration to the Convention’s 30th anniversary in 2002.

items.question: The Nara Document on Authenticity, cited in UNESCO’s World Heritage strategy timeline, was adopted in which year?
items.explanation: UNESCO’s convention timeline associates the Nara Document with 1994 Global Strategy work.

items.question: The UNESCO World Heritage Convention linking nature conservation and cultural property protection was adopted in which year?
items.explanation: UNESCO’s World Heritage Convention timeline dates adoption to 1972.

items.question: The World Heritage Convention was adopted by the UNESCO General Conference on which date in 1972?
items.explanation: UNESCO’s convention timeline records adoption on 16 November 1972.

items.question: The approach widely known as cultural geography in the United States was especially associated with which geographer’s Berkeley school?
items.explanation: Britannica ties U.S. cultural geography to Carl Sauer and the Berkeley school.

items.question: Which famous Indonesian monument appears in UNESCO’s World Heritage Convention page as an example of a major international safeguarding effort?
items.explanation: UNESCO’s World Heritage Convention narrative highlights restoration work at Borobudur.

items.question: Which site became among the first twelve inscribed on the World Heritage List in 1978?
items.explanation: UNESCO’s convention timeline lists Ecuador’s Galápagos Islands among the first inscriptions in 1978.

items.question: Which strategic objective—the fifth “C”—did the World Heritage Committee add in 2007?
items.explanation: UNESCO’s World Heritage materials describe adding “Communities” as the fifth “C” in 2007.

items.question: By the early 1980s, why did many countries want trade rules updated beyond the old GATT system?
items.correctAnswer: world trade had become more complex and services trade was not covered by GATT rules
incorrectAnswers: the moon was declared a free-trade zone
currency was fixed to silver worldwide
items.explanation: WTO history text notes growing complexity and that services trade lay outside GATT rules, prompting reform.

items.question: Economic geography links patterns of trade closely with which transport-focused field?
items.explanation: Britannica ties economic geography to transport geography when discussing trade patterns.

items.question: Economic geography’s traditional focus includes the distribution of what across regions?
items.explanation: Britannica’s human geography survey says economic geography studies the distribution of productive activities.

items.question: From 1948 to 1994, which agreement provided the core rules for much of world merchandise trade before the WTO?
items.explanation: The WTO’s history overview explains that GATT governed much of world trade from 1948 until the WTO era.

items.question: How many countries participated in the Tokyo Round of GATT negotiations, according to WTO’s round summary?
items.explanation: The WTO’s GATT rounds table lists 102 participating countries for the Tokyo Round.

items.question: Paul Krugman was awarded the Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences partly for work in which spatial field?
items.explanation: Britannica’s Krugman article notes the prize recognized his economic geography and trade-pattern work.

items.question: The Uruguay Round of multilateral trade negotiations ran from 1986 to which end year?
items.explanation: WTO tables list the Uruguay Round as 1986–1994.

items.question: The World Trade Organization began operating on which date?
items.explanation: WTO introductory materials state the WTO was created on 1 January 1995.

items.question: Which branch examines the spatial patterns of retail, finance, and other service sectors?
items.explanation: Britannica includes the geography of services among traditional economic geography topics.

items.question: Which economic geography subdivision deals with farming systems and agricultural land use?
items.explanation: Britannica lists the geography of agriculture among classic economic geography subdivisions.

items.question: Which subdivision focuses on factory location, industry clusters, and manufacturing patterns?
items.explanation: Britannica names industrial geography as a branch concerned with industrial activity patterns.

items.question: According to a UN Mother Language Day page citing UNESCO, about how many languages are spoken or signed globally?
items.explanation: The UN observance page cites UNESCO’s estimate of 8,324 spoken or signed languages.

items.question: Complete mastery of two languages is commonly termed what?
items.explanation: Britannica defines bilingualism as complete mastery of two languages.

items.question: How many official languages does the United Nations use for meetings and documentation?
items.explanation: The UN states there are six official languages: Arabic, Chinese, English, French, Russian, and Spanish.

items.question: International Mother Language Day is observed on which date?
items.explanation: UNESCO schedules International Mother Language Day on 21 February.

items.question: International Mother Language Day was first proclaimed by which organization?
items.explanation: UNESCO’s Mother Language Day page states UNESCO proclaimed the day, later welcomed by the UN General Assembly.

items.question: Mutually intelligible varieties that differ yet do not fully block understanding are often called what?
items.explanation: Britannica explains that substantially different but partly intelligible systems are called dialects of a language.

items.question: The idea to celebrate International Mother Language Day began as an initiative from which country?
items.explanation: UNESCO notes the observance was Bangladesh’s initiative.

items.question: The scientific study of language is known as what?
items.explanation: Britannica states the science of language is linguistics.

items.question: UN Language Days include both English Language Day and Spanish Language Day on which shared date?
items.explanation: The UN official languages page schedules English and Spanish Language Days on 23 April.

items.question: What term describes the habits of expression of a single speaker?
items.explanation: Britannica introduces the term idiolect for one person’s expressive habits.

items.question: Which of these is not one of the six official languages of the United Nations?
items.explanation: The UN lists Arabic, Chinese, English, French, Russian, and Spanish as its six official languages.

items.question: About what share of international migrants were female in 2024, per the UN overview?
items.explanation: The UN migration page reports female migrants as 48 per cent of the total in 2024.

items.question: According to the UN’s international migration overview, about how many international migrants were there worldwide in 2024?
items.explanation: UNDESA Population Division figures cited by the UN place the 2024 global migrant stock at about 304 million.

items.question: For UN statistics, who counts as an international migrant?
items.correctAnswer: any person who has changed his or her country of residence
items.explanation: The UN migration page defines an international migrant, for statistical purposes, as someone who changed country of residence.

items.question: In which year did IOM enter into an agreement to become a United Nations–related organization?
items.explanation: The UN migration page notes the 2016 agreement making IOM a related organization of the UN.

items.question: International Migrants Day is observed on which date each year?
items.explanation: The UN proclaimed 18 December as International Migrants Day.

items.question: International migrants made up about what share of the global population in 2024?
items.explanation: The UN migration page states migrants were about 3.7 per cent of the global population in 2024.

items.question: The International Organization for Migration (IOM) was established in which year?
items.explanation: The UN migration overview states IOM was established in 1951.

items.question: The intergovernmental conference that adopted the Global Compact for Migration was held in which city?
items.explanation: The UN migration page places the adopting conference in Marrakesh, Morocco.

items.question: The same UN overview estimates about how many international migrants there were worldwide in 1990?
items.explanation: The UN migration page cites roughly 154 million international migrants in 1990.

items.question: UN Member States adopted the Global Compact for Safe, Orderly and Regular Migration in which month and year?
items.explanation: The UN migration overview dates adoption of the Global Compact for Migration to December 2018.

items.question: Which Sustainable Development Goal target explicitly calls for orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration?
items.explanation: The UN migration page links SDG target 10.7 to well-managed migration policies.

items.question: According to the United Nations World Population Prospects 2024, which country has the largest population?
items.explanation: UN mid-2024 estimates rank India first globally in population size.

items.question: On what date did the United Nations mark the world population reaching eight billion?
items.explanation: The UN observed the Day of Eight Billion on 15 November 2022.

items.question: The U.S. Census Bureau nests nine census divisions inside how many census regions?
items.explanation: Four census regions group the states for statistical summaries.

items.question: Which African country has the largest population on the continent?
items.explanation: Nigeria is the most populous country in Africa.

items.question: Which East Asian island country has a large population despite limited arable land?
items.explanation: Japan is densely settled on its coastal plains and major urban corridors.

items.question: Which European Union member state has the largest population?
items.explanation: Germany is the most populous country in the European Union.

items.question: Which South American country is the continent’s most populous?
items.explanation: Brazil’s population is larger than that of any other South American state.

items.question: Which South Asian country has a very high population density on its fertile delta plains?
items.explanation: Bangladesh packs a large population into a compact riverine territory.

items.question: Which Southeast Asian archipelago country is among the world’s most populous states?
items.explanation: Indonesia ranks among the top countries globally in total population.

items.question: Which country in the Americas has the largest population?
items.explanation: The United States exceeds any other American country in population.

items.question: Which country lies along the Indus Valley and is among the world’s most populous states?
items.explanation: Pakistan is a densely peopled South Asian country draining the Indus system.

items.question: According to UN materials on SDG 11, about what percentage of people are expected to live in cities by 2050?
items.explanation: The UN SDG 11 page states that 70 per cent of people are expected to live in cities by 2050.

items.question: By 2030, about how many people out of 10 worldwide are expected to live in urban areas?
items.explanation: The UN human settlements overview cites a projection of about six in ten people in urban areas by 2030.

items.question: By the early 21st century, more than half of the world’s population lived where?
items.explanation: Britannica notes that by the early 21st century more than half of humanity lived in urban centres.

items.question: In which year did world leaders adopt the New Urban Agenda at Habitat III?
items.explanation: The UN SDG cities page links the New Urban Agenda to the 2016 Habitat III conference.

items.question: In which year was UN-Habitat established?
items.explanation: The UN human settlements overview states UN-Habitat was established in 1978.

items.question: The Habitat III conference, where the New Urban Agenda was adopted, was held in which city?
items.explanation: The UN SDG cities page states Habitat III took place in Quito, Ecuador, in October 2016.

items.question: The UN Sustainable Development Goal dedicated to inclusive, safe, resilient, and sustainable cities is Goal number?
items.explanation: SDG 11 is the cities and human settlements goal.

items.question: Urbanization is the process by which large numbers of people become permanently concentrated in relatively small areas, forming what?
items.explanation: Britannica defines urbanization as permanent concentration of people forming cities.

items.question: Which United Nations programme promotes socially and environmentally sustainable towns and cities?
items.explanation: UN-Habitat is the UN Human Settlements Programme focused on sustainable urbanization.

items.question: World Cities Day is observed on which calendar date?
items.explanation: The UN lists World Cities Day on 31 October.

items.question: World Habitat Day is celebrated on which recurring weekday?
items.explanation: The UN human settlements page schedules World Habitat Day on the first Monday of October.
